serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g, representing a generously portioned bowl containing fresh milk, grass jelly, lotus seeds, peach gum, red beans, and taro balls as served at a Singapore tong sui dessert stall.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: whole milk (NDB 01077), grass jelly (analogous to agar jelly), red beans cooked (NDB 16014), lotus seeds dried (NDB 16108), taro cooked (NDB 11514), and peach gum (collagen-type polysaccharide, negligible macro contribution); weighted by approximate ingredient proportions in a 400 g bowl.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite dish. Values are estimated by weighted-average composition of the six main components using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data. Approximate proportions assumed: fresh milk 45%, grass jelly 15%, red beans cooked 15%, lotus seeds cooked 10%, taro balls (taro-based) 10%, peach gum 5%. Taro balls contain tapioca starch and taro; modelled as cooked taro. Peach gum is primarily a polysaccharide gum with negligible protein and fat; modelled as contributing carbohydrate only at low proportion. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed legume and milk-based dessert bowls — analogous estimate based on: whole milk GI ~27 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr); red beans / adzuki beans GI ~35 (Foster-Powell et al. 2002, Am J Clin Nutr); taro GI ~54 (University of Sydney GI Database); grass jelly GI ~28 (analogous to agar jelly, University of Sydney GI Database); weighted composite GI estimated ~45.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Foster-Powell K, Holt SHA, Brand-Miller JC. Am J Clin Nutr 2002;76(1):5-56; Atkinson FS et al. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. GI estimated at 45 (low category) by weighted average of component GI values: whole milk (~27, 45% weight), red beans/adzuki (~35, 15% weight), taro (~54, 10% weight), grass jelly (~28, 15% weight), lotus seeds (~40 estimated, 10% weight), peach gum (~20 estimated polysaccharide, 5% weight). Glycemic load per 400 g serving calculated as (45 × 51.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 23.0 g. All component GI values sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed publications.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~28 mg/100 g; ~112 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 112 mg sodium per serving, this dish is suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though portion size should be monitored given the moderate carbohydrate and caloric load; no added salt is typical in this preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~23 g per 400 g serving) driven by taro balls and red beans
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic index of 45 (low) and glycemic load of approximately 23 g per serving indicate a moderate glycemic impact; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in controlled portions and account for the ~51 g total carbohydrate per serving within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100 g), dietary fibre from red beans and lotus seeds, and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content, presence of soluble dietary fibre from red beans and lotus seeds, and low sodium profile make this dish broadly compatible with a heart-healthy diet; the dairy component contributes modest saturated fat which should be considered in the context of total daily intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fresh Milk Ultimate Mix (鲜奶极品什锦) is a Chinese-Singaporean tong sui (糖水) dessert bowl served at specialty dessert stalls in Singapore. The dish features a generous assembly of six main components: fresh whole milk as the liquid base, grass jelly (仙草冻) cut into cubes, premium Hunan lotus seeds (莲子) steamed until soft and melt-in-the-mouth, peach gum (桃胶) soaked and set into translucent pearls, cooked red beans (红豆), and chewy taro balls (芋圆) made from taro and tapioca starch. The bowl is served cold or at room temperature. The flavour profile is mildly sweet and creamy from the fresh milk, with earthy and slightly bitter notes from the grass jelly, a delicate floral sweetness from the peach gum, and a starchy, chewy contrast from the taro balls and lotus seeds. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 72 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate carbohydrate content (~12.8 g/100 g), modest protein (~2.1 g/100 g) largely from milk and legumes, and low fat (~1.6 g/100 g). The dietary fibre from red beans and lotus seeds (~1.4 g/100 g) contributes to satiety. The estimated glycemic index is 45 (low category), making it a relatively lower-GI dessert option compared to many sweetened confections, though the glycemic load per 400 g serving is approximately 23 g and should be accounted for by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is vegetarian and halal-friendly. It is culturally associated with traditional Chinese herbal dessert culture and is popular in Singapore's dessert stall scene, particularly among diners seeking a refreshing, lightly sweet, and texturally complex cold dessert.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite multi-ingredient Chinese dessert bowl. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of the six main components using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data. GI is estimated from component GI values sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed publications. Confidence is medium due to the estimation methodology and the absence of a direct match in any primary regional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA).
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on approximate ingredient proportions (fresh milk 45%, grass jelly 15%, red beans cooked 15%, lotus seeds cooked 10%, taro balls 10%, peach gum 5%) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Actual restaurant portions may vary. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed zero as the dish contains no hydrogenated fats by ingredient composition. 3. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.4), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1) are estimated from the milk and legume fat profiles in USDA SR Legacy. 5. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported or calculable from the component-level USDA SR Legacy data used for this estimation; specifically: no -1 values remain in this record as all fields have been populated with estimates or confirmed values. 6. Peach gum (桃胶) is a tree resin polysaccharide; its macro contribution is modelled as carbohydrate only with negligible protein and fat, consistent with available food science literature. 7. The food_group is retained as 'Beverages' per the input source classification, though this dish is more accurately a dessert bowl; food_subgroup clarifies this distinction. 8. Price data (SGD 11.60) is preserved from the field survey data source record for reference only and is not used as a nutritional evidence source.
